text: Why does more structure make you creative, not less?
What I learned through time is that structure and creativity aren't polar opposites.
They actually feed into one another.
If you're really creative and have no structure, you'll probably be a one-hit wonder.
And if you have a lot of structure and no creativity, you won't be unique and authentic.
And so the way that you strike that perfect balance is by adding structure to your creativity.
Which basically means, what do you do once you actually come up with an idea?
The idea and coming up with it is the creative part.
Everything else should be a system.
You should come up with a framework or something that you follow every single time, in order to go from idea to actual execution.
That way you know that you can just crank out ideas and that they actually get implemented.
